name: At581X → Settings
description: > [!WARNING] > The hardware frontend reset option is only required to reset the frontend in case it is struck, before sending the > new configuration. However, a frontend reset is always performed after changing the settings.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID of the sensor on which settings should be changed. If only one radar is defined, this is optional.

config_entries.label: Hw Frontend Reset
config_entries.description: If set to true, a reset of the analog frontend will be performed before changing other options. Ignored if not set or set to `false`. Upon applying the settings a frontend reset will be performed anyway, this is only useful if the sensor is not answering or locked up.

config_entries.label: Poweron Selfcheck Time
config_entries.description: The delay to perform self check and calibration on power on. Recommended not to change this

config_entries.label: Protect Time
config_entries.description: The delay after an end-of-trigger event where the detection will not trigger anymore. Max 65535ms

config_entries.label: Sensing Distance
config_entries.description: A unitless number, in range 0-1023, specifying the maximum distance to detect motion

config_entries.label: Stage Gain
config_entries.description: The analog gain to use for threshold test. Any value in range 0-12, with 12 being the lowest gain and 0 the highest

config_entries.label: Trigger Base
config_entries.description: The delay while a detection must be active to change the state of the sensor. Max 65535ms

config_entries.label: Trigger Keep
config_entries.description: The delay that the output will stay high after a detection event. This is usually what you want to change.
